---
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: letter
    geometry:
      - top=1in
      - bottom=1in
      - left=1in
      - right=1in
    fontsize: 12pt
    mainfont: "Helvetica Neue"
    linestretch: 1.0
    number-sections: false
    toc: false
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: NavyBlue
    urlcolor: NavyBlue
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage{lineno}
        \pagenumbering{gobble}
        \renewcommand\linenumberfont{\normalfont\normalsize}
        \newlength{\clauseindent}
        \setlength{\clauseindent}{1.15in}
        \tolerance=1000
        \hyphenpenalty=500
        \exhyphenpenalty=500
        \emergencystretch=1em
        \newcommand{\clause}[2]{%
          \par\noindent
          \hangindent=\clauseindent\hangafter=1%
          \makebox[\clauseindent][l]{#1}%
          #2\par}
        \newcommand{\blankline}{\par\noindent\strut\par}
---

```{=latex}
\begin{flushright}
Adopted: XX/XX/20XX
\end{flushright}

\begin{center}
{\Large\bfseries ACADEMIC SENATE}\\[4pt]
{\bfseries of}\\[4pt]
{\large\bfseries CALIFORNIA POLYTECHNIC STATE UNIVERSITY}\\[4pt]
{\bfseries San Luis Obispo, CA}\\[1.5em]
{\bfseries AS-XXX-26}\\[1em]
{\large\bfseries RESOLUTION ON STUDENT PERCEPTIONS OF LEARNING EXPERIENCE}
\end{center}

\vspace{1em}

\linenumbers

\clause{WHEREAS,}{In 2013, the Academic Senate adopted AS-759-13, establishing the current university-wide student evaluation questions: (1) ``Overall, this instructor was educationally effective'' and (2) ``Overall, this course was educationally effective''; and}

\blankline

\clause{WHEREAS,}{In 2025, ASI Resolution \#25-04 called for reform of the student evaluation instrument and processes, citing concerns about bias, validity, and the impact of evaluations on faculty from underrepresented groups; and}

\blankline

\clause{WHEREAS,}{The Academic Senate established the Ad Hoc Committee on Student Perception of Teaching Effectiveness, charged with providing a revised policy and resolution to replace AS-759-13; and}

\blankline

\clause{WHEREAS,}{A substantial body of peer-reviewed research establishes that student evaluations of teaching have little connection to teaching quality; have large unpredictable biases from the instructor's gender, race, and other characteristics protected under employment law; reflect grade expectations rather than learning; and are sometimes negatively associated with teaching effectiveness (Stark, 2026); and}

\blankline

\clause{WHEREAS,}{Research establishes that open-ended comments in student evaluations show the strongest evidence of equity bias (Kreitzer and Sweet-Cushman, 2021); and}

\blankline

\clause{WHEREAS,}{Assessments of disciplinary competence, pedagogical technique, and achievement of learning outcomes require perspectives beyond what students can observe from within a single course---and evidence suggests that student perceptions of their own learning are inconsistent with actual learning (Deslauriers et al., 2019; Uttl, White, and Gonzalez, 2017); and}

\blankline

\clause{WHEREAS,}{There is a broader movement toward multidimensional evaluation of teaching, which recognizes that student surveys should focus on dimensions students are qualified to assess with minimal bias, as part of a comprehensive evaluation system (TEval Project, 2025; Austin et al., 2025); and}

\blankline

\clause{WHEREAS,}{The NSF-funded TEval initiative identifies seven dimensions for the multidimensional evaluation of teaching: (1) Goals, Content, and Alignment; (2) Teaching Practices; (3) Class Climate; (4) Achievement of Learning Outcomes; (5) Reflection and Iterative Growth; (6) Mentoring and Advising; and (7) Involvement in Teaching Service, Scholarship, or Community; therefore, be it}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That AS-759-13 is superseded upon implementation of this resolution; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate approve that the university-wide student evaluation instrument be redesigned to ask only about aspects of the learning experience that students are qualified to assess with minimal bias, avoiding items that frame students as evaluators of teaching effectiveness, course effectiveness, or instructor competence; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate approve that the instrument, once redesigned, be named the Student Perceptions of Learning Experience; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate approve that the instrument assess the following six aspects of class climate (Dimension 3 of the TEval framework): Regard for Students, Consistent Communication and Enforcement of Expectations, Access to Instructor and Instructor Resources, Perceived Course Coherence, Participatory Climate, and Responsive Learning Environment; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate approve that any open-ended questions on the instrument be tied to specific aspects of class climate through structured prompts, and be accompanied by an informational anti-bias preamble; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate implement the Ad Hoc Committee's report ``Student Perceptions of Learning Experience: Rationale and Broad Principles of Design'' and its appendices on scoring and reporting, and implementation; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate establish a standing committee with broad representation charged with oversight of all student evaluation instruments and initiatives---summative, formative, and department-originated---including future revisions to the instrument, subject to approval by the Academic Senate; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate forward the companion document ``Formative Learning Feedback: A Companion to the Student Perceptions of Learning Experience Report'', prepared by a sub-committee of this Ad Hoc Committee, to the Academic Senate Instruction Committee and to the Center for Teaching, Learning and Technology for their consideration; and be it further}

\blankline

\clause{RESOLVED:}{That the Academic Senate forward the companion document ``Guidance for Evaluation of Instruction'' (formatted as proposed language for UFPP \S8.3), prepared by a sub-committee of this Ad Hoc Committee, to the Academic Senate Faculty Affairs Committee for their consideration.}

\nolinenumbers

\vspace{2em}
\noindent
\begin{tabular}{@{}ll}
Proposed by: & Ad Hoc Committee on Student Perception\\
             & of Teaching Effectiveness\\[0.5em]
Date:        & \textit{[date of first reading]}\\
\end{tabular}
```
